Package com.enovka.gemini4j.domain.type

Enum HarmBlockThresholdEnum

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.lang.Enum<HarmBlockThresholdEnum>
      • com.enovka.gemini4j.domain.type.HarmBlockThresholdEnum
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<HarmBlockThresholdEnum>
    public enum HarmBlockThresholdEnum
extends Enum<HarmBlockThresholdEnum>
    Defines the harm probability thresholds that determine when the Gemini API should block content based on its safety assessment. These thresholds allow developers to control the sensitivity of the content filtering system, specifying the minimum harm probability level that triggers content blocking. By choosing the appropriate `HarmBlockThresholdEnum` value, developers can fine-tune the balance between safety and permissiveness in their applications.
    Author:
    Everson Novka <enovka@gmail.com>

    • Enum Constant Summary

      Enum Constants 
      Enum Constant Description
      BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE
      **Block Low and Above:** Block content with a harm probability of `LOW`, `MEDIUM`, or `HIGH`.
      BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE
      **Block Medium and Above:** Block content with a harm probability of `MEDIUM` or `HIGH`.
      BLOCK_NONE
      **Block None:** Do not block any content based on harm probability.
      BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH
      **Block Only High:** Block only content with a `HIGH` harm probability.
      HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED
      **Unspecified Harm Block Threshold:** The harm block threshold is unspecified.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      static HarmBlockThresholdEnum valueOf​(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name.
      static HarmBlockThresholdEnum[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.

    • Enum Constant Detail

      • HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED

        public static final HarmBlockThresholdEnum HARM_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_UNSPECIFIED
        **Unspecified Harm Block Threshold:** The harm block threshold is unspecified. This value should not be used. Always choose a specific threshold from the other options (`BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE`, `BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE`, `BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH`, or `BLOCK_NONE`) to ensure predictable behavior.

      • BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE

        public static final HarmBlockThresholdEnum BLOCK_LOW_AND_ABOVE
        **Block Low and Above:** Block content with a harm probability of `LOW`, `MEDIUM`, or `HIGH`. This threshold enforces a stricter level of content filtering, blocking content with even a low probability of harm.

      • BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE

        public static final HarmBlockThresholdEnum BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE
        **Block Medium and Above:** Block content with a harm probability of `MEDIUM` or `HIGH`. This threshold offers a moderate level of content filtering, blocking content with a moderate to high probability of harm.

      • BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH

        public static final HarmBlockThresholdEnum BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH
        **Block Only High:** Block only content with a `HIGH` harm probability. This threshold provides a more permissive level of content filtering, allowing content with low and medium harm probabilities.

      • BLOCK_NONE

        public static final HarmBlockThresholdEnum BLOCK_NONE
        **Block None:** Do not block any content based on harm probability. This threshold disables content blocking based on the safety assessment, allowing all content to be returned regardless of its harm probability.

    • Method Detail

      • values

        public static HarmBlockThresholdEnum[] values()
        Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared. This method may be used to iterate over the constants as follows: 
        for (HarmBlockThresholdEnum c : HarmBlockThresholdEnum.values())
    System.out.println(c);
        Returns:
        an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

      • valueOf

        public static HarmBlockThresholdEnum valueOf​(String name)
        Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
        Returns:
        the enum constant with the specified name
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
        NullPointerException - if the argument is null
